title: gen-published.mts nondeterministic: each build rewrites published/ with fresh ULIDs (300 file changes/run)
description: Running 'pnpm --filter viewer build' (prebuild gen-published.mts) regenerates apps/viewer/public/published/ with new ULID history ids: 100 deleted + 100 modified + 100 untracked files vs HEAD. Committed artifacts can never match a rebuild; any build dirties the tree. Fix: derive stable ids (content-hash or seeded) or stop committing generated history files. Found during /goal cycle 1 (2026-06-09).

title: og:image for published exhibits needs an absolute deploy origin (PUBLISH_BASE/astro site)
description: UNBLOCKED by ADR-0013 (+amendment: archie.config.json). DECIDED (grill 2026-06-09): og:image = the exhibit's cover resolved ABSOLUTE — IIIF-sourced covers pass through sized via the Image API (~1200w, e.g. /full/1200,/0/default.jpg — museum infra, most durable URLs in the system); local-asset covers prefixed with canonicalOrigin at build; no-cover pages fall back to ONE static 1200x630 brand card (favicon mark on dark canvas) at the canonical origin. Per-exhibit composited cards (title+cover) parked as a possible later upgrade — needs an image build dep, marginal at zero users.
closeReason: outcome:success — og:image shipped (cover-absolute + brand card) under the archie.config.json origin source

title: Contributor-broadening ①: local image folder → in-browser IIIF manifest
description: ① local folder -> exhibit. Slice A SHIPPED (e2bb2be: folder gesture, natural order, existing ingest). DECIDED (grill 2026-06-09): slice B = ONE EXHIBIT PER SUBFOLDER (matches 'each box is an exhibit'; loose top-level files become an exhibit named for the root folder), folded together with ⑫ EXIF capture-date (DateTimeOriginal read at import -> photo folders sort by shot time, not filename; bake.ts already parses EXIF headers). One folder-import-depth cycle, queued after the canonical-wiring cycle.
closeReason: outcome:success — ① complete per decided scope: slice A folder gesture (e2bb2be), slice B one-exhibit-per-subfolder + ⑫ EXIF shot-time order (20615c9). Undecided future ideas (metadata sidecars, manifest export beyond publish) were never queued; new seeds if ever wanted.

title: Contributor-broadening ②: IIIF manifest URL paste import
description: RESEARCH-BACKED. One paste bootstraps from 50k+ institutional IIIF collections. Donor: cozy-iiif parseURL (PURE, Prior Art/14). Lowest-friction on-ramp + portable WADM. P1/P2/P4. Effort S-M. Fits frame.
closeReason: outcome:success — IIIF manifest URL paste import landed (P2+P3, service-base preferred, AV+duration, collection refusal). e2e: Archie's own published manifest round-trips to a 12-object exhibit.

title: Contributor-broadening ③: segment-diverse Playground templates
description: RESEARCH-BACKED ③ + absorbs ⑬ (grill, 2026-06-09). Template = 'Where Languages Go Silent': UNESCO Atlas of the World's Languages in Danger (IA atlas-of-the-worlds-languages-in-danger, CC BY-SA 4.0, IIIF verified; shortlist /tmp/archie-a11y/ia-template-shortlist.md). 8-10 regional map pages; annotations pin endangered-language dots (speaker counts, causes, a phrase). MUST ship with TWO READINGS — 'Linguist's reading' (classification/speaker data) vs 'Community reading' (what the silence means locally) — making Archie's biggest differentiator (rival interpretations; absent from all 21 surveyed tools) legible in a non-manuscript register. ⑬ dedicated-UI work reassessed only after this ships. RULE: no personal work as bundled examples. HUMAN-GATED: author on a branch; user reviews content before merge.
closeReason: outcome:success — 'Where Languages Go Silent' merged by user review (2765c83): 8 UNESCO atlas map pages via IA IIIF, CC BY-SA carried per object, two Readings (Linguist's/Community) demonstrating the differentiator (⑬ folded in). Region boxes approximate — refine in place if users notice.

title: Contributor-broadening ④: surface ?src=<zip-url> as first-class publish path
description: RESEARCH-BACKED. Zero-GitHub publish path. Non-specialists (P3/P7/P8) won't make a GitHub account; GH-Pages publish is a hard ceiling. Mechanism exists (CONTEXT §224) — needs UX elevation + link-rot warning. Effort S-M. Fits frame.
closeReason: outcome:success — ?src= share path surfaced in the publish dialog with ADR-0009 durability caveat; review-hardened (no phantom-save claims, clipboard fallback, URL validation).

title: Contributor-broadening ⑤: build-time SEO / og-tags / JSON-LD / sitemap
description: RESEARCH-BACKED. Discoverability is word-of-mouth today. Astro build step: og:image (cover canvas), Schema.org CreativeWork JSON-LD, @astrojs/sitemap. Every exhibit findable + social-unfurls. Zero runtime. All authors. Effort S. 03-system-design §2.5.

title: Contributor-broadening ⑥: CSV/spreadsheet → annotation bulk import
description: RESEARCH-BACKED. Author annotations in Excel/Sheets; zero annotation-UI learning curve. Final-mile exhibit layer on FromThePage/Transkribus CSV. Donor: field-studio exceljs (Prior Art/14). Pure JS. P1/P4/P5. Effort M. Fits frame.

If that host moves, the bundled example breaks. Options: vendor a downscaled map into studio public/ (like fonts, ADR-0012 precedent) or add a broken-media notice tailored for templates. Same applies to voynich (Yale) — policy decision, one approach for all templates.

Hand-rolled src/pages/sitemap.xml.ts consuming archie.config.json + robots.txt pointing at it. No @astrojs/sitemap dep.

title: PARKED: classroom batch zip import — SPLIT from the server-shaped inbox (grill 2026-06-09)
description: Grill verdict: the synthesis item 'submission inbox / batch zip import' is two things. (1) Submission inbox (central upload target) = genuinely server-shaped, NEVER BUILD. (2) Batch LOCAL zip import (teacher already holds N student zips; imports them all; each becomes a Reading — 'rival student takes' — or a merge pass via existing mergeParents/MergeReview machinery) = buildable, NOT server-shaped — but PARKED by user. Revive trigger: a real educator workflow shows up. When revived, grill the model fork first: Reading-per-student vs merge-pass-per-student. Effort M-L.

title: PARKED: server-shaped ledger — volunteer queue, cross-library discovery, DID signing (grill 2026-06-09)
description: Consolidated dispositions for the post-Tier-2 server-shaped items (GOAL §5a 'do NOT build'): (1) Volunteer task queue — never build (shared claim-state = a server); approximation pointer = Tier-2 GitHub-Issues intake (claims live in issue assignment). (2) Cross-library discovery — never build the registry; approximation is CONTENT not engineering: a curated gallery-of-galleries links page on the canonical instance, when real published libraries exist to link. (3) DID signing — never build, NO approximation queued (rights/credit fields + git history are the v1 provenance story; unsigned half-measures worse than honesty); revisit only if scholarly citation demands cryptographic provenance. Related: batch-zip-import split = Archie-f1e2; live co-editing approximation = the ⑧ summary panel (Archie-59a8).

title: og:image upsize assumes a level-2 IIIF server — build-time probe (review r8 nit)
description: The blind /full/1200,/ upsize works on level-2 servers (Yale) but would 404 on level-0, unfurling with NO image (the brand-card fallback catches non-matching URLs, not dead ones). When templates from other hosts land (e.g. the IA Atlas), add a build-time HEAD probe of the upsized cover; fall back to the original cover size or the brand card on failure.

title: 0.3 cut 3: extract view-state store from App.svelte — do WITH marginalia (worklist 2.1)
description: Cuts 1-2 done (binding-store.svelte.ts, publish-flows.svelte.ts; App.svelte 2013→1792 lines, 77/77 tests). The remaining canonical view-state store (selected/editing/creating/view/currentSlug/currentObjectId) reshapes the same state the marginalia system (worklist 2.1) needs — do them together to avoid reworking the seam twice. See docs/plans/IMPROVEMENT-WORKLIST.md.

title: 0.4(c): always-green browser suite on author-save-publish-read loop
description: Worklist 0.4 remainder: small Playwright/lightpanda suite covering the core loop (create note → autosave → publish locally → Viewer renders it). Unit-level pieces landed in-session: save-queue tests, gesture-guard tests, session degenerate-guard tests, binding helpers.

title: 1.1 remainder: pin/dot LOD morphing + note positions in the locator (browser-verified)
description: Landed in-session: OSD navigator locator (mount opts, verified on openseadragon@5.0.1), zoom-band stamping (data-archie-zoom far/mid/near, pure zoomBand() tested) and CSS weight via screen-space channels. Remainder needs browser verification: true pin/dot morphing at far zoom (custom overlay, not stylable on Annotorious shapes) and note-position dots inside the navigator. Also browser-verify navigator chrome fit with the curator's-study aesthetic.

title: openExhibit does not flush a pending debounced save before swapping session
description: Pre-existing hazard noticed during worklist 0.1: scheduleSave debounces 800ms; openExhibit/switchObject replaces session+annDir without clearTimeout+flush, so an edit made <800ms before switching exhibits can write against the OLD captured session/dir (queue serializes the write but the timing window remains) or be lost if the timer fires after annDir changed. Fix: flush (clearTimeout + await save()) at the top of openExhibit. App.svelte ~line 211/267.
closeReason: outcome:success — flush (clearTimeout+await save) added at openExhibit; timer cancelled in replaceProjectFrom; studio 87/87 + build green

title: Scale cue: zoom magnitude indicator on the canvas
description: Honest-map item (affordance critique): the locator shows WHERE, nothing shows HOW FAR IN — add a quiet scale indicator (e.g. '3.2×' or a scale bar) to the canvas chrome, both surfaces. Browser-verified work.

title: Touch design pass: markers/popovers/drawing assume mouse
description: Honest-map item: hover-discovery, popover anchoring, and draw gestures have no touch design. Needs a deliberate pass (tap targets, long-press vs drag semantics, popover placement on small viewports).

title: Accessibility pass: screen-reader walk + overlay contrast gate
description: Honest-map item: aria/keyboard exist piecemeal; no SR testing has ever been done. Overlay contrast is v1.1-gated (CONTEXT orphan gap 4) — schedule before first institutional pilot.

title: Library-scoped Object IDs (soften ADR-0001 drift)
description: Retrospective honorable mention: keep Object IDs library-scoped even while exhibit-nested so a later shared-pool migration is a projection change, not an ID rewrite through annotation targets. Touches model + canvasId grammar — needs a plan.

title: Split CONTEXT.md: stable Language vs append-only decision ledger
description: Honest-map item: CONTEXT.md (245 lines) is both glossary and ledger; every decision amends it. Split needs care — 'CONTEXT §N' references exist throughout docs/code comments. Propose: extract Language into LANGUAGE.md, leave anchored pointer + section map in CONTEXT.md. User-gate before doing (hottest doc in the repo).

title: 4.1 Validation: one real exhibit through author→publish→read→cite with outside readers
description: Honest-map item, worklist Phase 4 keystone: every invention gates against a real exhibit with real visitors, not bundled templates. Blocked-ish on Phase 2 UI being usable; schedule deliberately.

title: 2.2 Edit at the locus (popover becomes the editor)
description: Worklist 2.2 / MARGINALIA-PLAN cut E: WADM form renders inside the focused margin card; sidebar becomes pure index; amend ADR-0006 wording. After marginalia cuts A-D.
closeReason: outcome:success — edit-at-locus shipped: WADM form renders inside the focused MARGIN CARD for image objects (popover remains the AV/narrative surface by design); Playwright-verified (verify-marginalia.mjs, in-card edit lands in preview)

title: 2.3 Readings rail (layers panel) — blocked on overlap decision record
description: Worklist 2.3: swatch/name/count/visibility toggles/solo-on-hover panel replacing the dropdown+radio. The overlap affordance needs the Q5 decision proposal (see docs/decisions/proposals) user-gated first. Three reworks of Readings UI in one week = no home; this is the home.
closeReason: outcome:success — ReadingsRail shipped: visible-set toggles + active-pen radio (Q1 split), comparing=outline-only via shared readingMarkerStyle (Q2), rail=use/modal=edit, dropdown retired (Q3). reading-state model 11 tests; marker-style 6 tests; browser harness 9/9 (screenshots for the human comprehension gate). Found+fixed real bug: stable styleOf identity froze restyling. Viewer v1 lock verified (legend still radio).

title: 3.1 Surfaces follow attention, not geography
description: Worklist 3.1, deliberately after 2.1/2.2: paper becomes the material of the attended note (selected card brightest), dark stays ambient; amber = dark accent, green = paper ink. The palette problems dissolve as corollaries.

title: DECISION P-1: archivability of published exhibits (hash routes vs citation mission)
description: Proposal drafted at docs/decisions/PROPOSALS.md §P-1. Recommend option A (noscript/sitemap projection at publish) now, B (per-note citation HTML) v1.1. User gate via /triage; on approval mint Q-N in archie-linkability scope.
closeReason: outcome:success — P-1 grilled with user (3 Qs resolved: artifact-level, per-note anchors, pipeline-reuse rendering); minted archie-linkability Q-2 + ADR-0014; CONTEXT.md durable-ref term added

title: DECISION P-2: readings display-composition vs Q5 exclusivity (rail prerequisite)
description: Proposal drafted at docs/decisions/PROPOSALS.md §P-2. Key separation: membership exclusivity (Q5, untouched) vs display exclusivity (UI composition). Recommend A (Studio rail multi-visibility, Viewer stays radio) now, B (opt-in Viewer compare) v1.1. Blocks Archie-113b. User gate via /triage.
closeReason: outcome:success — P-2 grilled with user (3 Qs resolved: visible-vs-active split, outline-only comparing, rail=use/modal=edit/dropdown retired); minted archie-ux Q-2; CONTEXT.md terms added; Archie-113b (rail build) now UNBLOCKED

title: Marginalia presentation redesign (UI reverted 2026-06-11; engine retained)
description: User reviewed the live marginalia (cuts D-F) and reverted it: 'does not look good.' The UI wiring is out of both apps; the classic list+popover is restored and Playwright-confirmed. RETAINED, headless-tested and inert: core layoutMarginalia(+pinId guaranteed placement), mount markerScreenRects, Canvas rectIds/onmarkerrects plumbing, render-svelte MarginColumn.svelte (unconsumed, tree-shaken), scripts/verify-marginalia.mjs (parked harness, will fail until re-wired). Next attempt should start from a DESIGN pass (what specifically looked wrong: card chrome? leader? motion? density?) before re-wiring — gather that before building.

title: CI prebuild clobbers user-committed exhibits in public/published/
description: apps/viewer/package.json prebuild runs gen-published.mts with no --from; the script rmSync's public/published/ and regenerates from sample-data. A cloner who commits authored exhibits has them silently wiped at deploy. Fix direction: prebuild consumes committed .archie.zip(s) from a conventional path, or skips regen when a non-sample tree exists.
closeReason: outcome:success — gen is merge-preserving (core mergePublishedIndexes + targeted per-exhibit clean) AND deterministic (seeded rng in sample-data log builders); CI prebuild can no longer clobber user-committed exhibits, and regens are byte-identical

title: gen-published.mts --from bakes sample-data BASE into user manifests
description: gen-published.mts:37 passes { baseUrl: BASE } from ../src/sample-data.js to publishLibrary even when sourcing a user .archie.zip via --from — user manifests may get the sample image base. baseUrl should derive from the loaded library / config.

title: SNAG: zoom-band (1.1) and arrival-pulse (1.3) CSS are INERT — Annotorious 3 renders marks to WebGL canvas
description: Browser probe 2026-06-11 (verify-readings-rail work): annotation layer is canvas.a9s-gl-canvas; svg.a9s-annotationlayer has NO per-annotation children, so CSS targeting .a9s-annotation (Canvas.svelte zoom-band weights, Reader.svelte arrival breathe, and the pre-existing drop-shadow rule) styles nothing. Redo both through the JS style channel: zoom band → modulate readingMarkerStyle via setStyle on zoom-band change; arrival pulse → timed style-expression sweep (or OSD overlay). The comparing regime is UNAFFECTED (it already rides DrawingStyleExpression).
